message:
fix(test): correct mcp-agent-mail fake tool version string

The fake mcp-agent-mail tool was outputting "am 1.0.0" instead of
"mcp-agent-mail 1.0.0", which was inconsistent with the binary name
that the AMAdapter looks for.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,7 +1,6 @@
 #!/bin/bash
-# Fake am tool for testing (Agent Mail launcher)
-# Note: Agent Mail is typically accessed via MCP, not CLI
-# This fake simulates the launcher script
+# Fake mcp-agent-mail tool for testing (Agent Mail MCP server binary)
+# This is the actual binary name the AMAdapter looks for
 
 MODE="${FAKE_TOOL_MODE:-normal}"
 
@@ -10,15 +9,15 @@ case "$MODE" in
         sleep 3600
         ;;
     error)
-        echo "am: simulated error" >&2
+        echo "mcp-agent-mail: simulated error" >&2
         exit 1
         ;;
 esac
 
 # Normal mode - check arguments
 case "$1" in
     --version)
-        echo "am 1.0.0 (fake)"
+        echo "mcp-agent-mail 1.0.0 (fake)"
         exit 0
         ;;
     status)
@@ -34,16 +33,16 @@ case "$1" in
         exit 0
         ;;
     --help|-h)
-        echo "am - Agent Mail launcher (fake for testing)"
-        echo "Usage: am [command] [options]"
+        echo "mcp-agent-mail - Agent Mail MCP server (fake for testing)"
+        echo "Usage: mcp-agent-mail [command] [options]"
         echo "Commands:"
         echo "  status    Check server status"
         echo "  start     Start the server"
         echo "  stop      Stop the server"
         exit 0
         ;;
     *)
-        echo "am: unknown command: $1" >&2
+        echo "mcp-agent-mail: unknown command: $1" >&2
         exit 1
         ;;
 esac
